title: foundation — model traits
description: The trait contract every downstream module composes on top of. Foundation owns 9 traits and 8 paired migration macros. Each trait describes: the columns it adds, the query scopes it introduces, the event hooks it registers, the relations it opens, the backing package (when third-party), and the models it's typically applied to. The `compositionExample` at the bottom shows the canonical trait order every persistent model uses. NOTE: `Importable` / `Exportable` (previously listed here) moved to `modules/transfer/` — the CSV/Excel/PDF/JSON import + export substrate now owns its own attribute-driven registration + queue chain + notification lifecycle. Consumer modules that opted into imports/exports switch to `Academorix\Transfer\Concerns\HasImportable` / `HasExportable`. `Searchable` (previously listed here) moved to `modules/search/` on Wave 6 inception — the search substrate now owns its own attribute-driven registration + engine adapters (Meilisearch / PG FTS / pgvector / ES) + zero-downtime reindex via alias swap + synonyms + saved queries + analytics. Consumer modules that opted into search switch to `Academorix\Search\Concerns\HasSearchable`.

owned.description: Marks rows the platform seeded (as opposed to rows the customer created). `is_system=true` rows are refused on Policy@update / @delete. Prevents workspace admins from accidentally editing or deleting platform-owned records (default business types, seeded permissions, system integrations).
notAppliedBy: Domain / DomainRecord / Branding — no platform-seeded rows

owned.description: Adds the userstamp trio (created_by / updated_by / deleted_by). Values auto-filled from the resolved caller on save / update / soft-delete. UUID FK to `users.id` (nullable for platform-initiated writes and seeded rows).

columns.name: deleted_by
columns.description: Set only when SoftDeletes is also composed

eventHooks.description: If created_at is null, set created_by = auth()->id(). Always set updated_by = auth()->id().
When SoftDeletes is composed, set deleted_by = auth()->id() before the delete cascade.

appliedBy: essentially every persistent model in every module
notAppliedBy: InvitationEvent (dropped in refactor — was immutable log)
InvitationEvent-style append-only audit rows in other modules

owned.description: Wraps `owen-it/laravel-auditing`. Writes an audit row to the `audits` table on every mutation with workspace_id + user_id + attribute diff. The audit table itself lives in the `audit` module; this trait writes to it via the wrapper.

eventHooks.description: Writes an audit row via the auditing package's default listener.

configuration.auditInclude: Allowlist of attributes on the model (opt-in over opt-out for security).
configuration.auditExclude: Denylist for models where an allowlist is impractical (secrets always excluded automatically).
configuration.auditTimestamps: false by default — timestamps produce audit noise.

appliedBy: Application / Workspace / Domain / Branding / WorkspaceContact / WorkspaceIntegration (workspaces)
any model touching PII or entitlement-affecting state

owned.description: Row-level version history — full state snapshots on every save with rollback + structured diffing. Wraps `mpociot/versionable`. Every mutation writes a snapshot of the model state (JSON blob) to the polymorphic `versions` table + provides `revertToVersion(n)`, `previousVersion()`, `currentVersion()`, `getVersions()`, `diffVersions(from, to)`. Distinct from HasAuditable: auditable stores diffs + actor for compliance evidence, HasVersions stores FULL SNAPSHOTS for rollback UX. Both traits coexist because they answer different questions — auditable answers 'who changed what', HasVersions answers 'restore to how it looked at 3pm yesterday'.

eventHooks.description: Writes a Version row snapshot of the model state into the polymorphic `versions` table (owned by `mpociot/versionable`). Excludes fields listed in $dontVersionFields.
When revertToVersion(n) is called, hydrates the model from the snapshot's `model_data` JSON and saves (which itself creates a new version).

configuration.dontVersionFields: Array property on the model: fields to exclude from snapshots (passwords, remember_token, updated_at). Foundation's default excludes: password, remember_token, api_token, cache_column.
configuration.keepOldVersions: int property on the model: max versions to retain (0 = unlimited). Foundation's convention: 50 for workspace-authored content, 200 for template-like models, unlimited for audit-critical models.
configuration.versioningEnabled: bool property (or static override). Set false to disable snapshotting for a specific model (rare — typically transient data).

appliedBy: Branding (workspaces) — revert to prior visual identity
notAppliedBy: NotificationPreference — preferences change frequently; snapshot noise
User (user) — profile changes tracked via HasAuditable; full snapshot leaks PII

owned.description: Adds a free-form `metadata` JSONB column + `array` cast + fluent accessors. NEVER queried against — metadata is opaque to the database engine. Use it for platform notes, integration references, ticket IDs, migration source tags, provisioner tags, canary bucket assignments — anything that doesn't earn a first-class column.
appliedBy: every persistent model in every module — this is the universal escape hatch for platform-flavoured data that shouldn't earn a schema column

owned.description: Adds a `sort_order` unsigned int column + sortable query scope + drag-and-drop reorder helpers.

compositeIndexes.description: When Sortable is applied to a scoped list, the composite index is generated.

eventHooks.description: Assign sort_order = max(sort_order) + 1 within the sort group when not explicitly set.

owned.description: Integration with `spatie/laravel-query-builder`. Reads a static `filterable(): array` method on the model.
appliedBy: every model with a listing endpoint
owned.usageExample: public static function filterable(): array { return [ 'allowedFilters' => [AllowedFilter::exact('state'), AllowedFilter::partial('email')], 'allowedSorts' => ['created_at', 'expires_at'], 'defaultSort' => '-created_at' ]; }

owned.description: Adds a `slug` string column with a unique index + auto-fills on save.

eventHooks.description: If slug is empty, generate from sluggableSource().

owned.description: v2 STUB. Adds a pgvector `embeddings` column of configurable dimension + a `nearest($vector, $count)` scope for cosine-similarity queries. Deferred until AI features land.

eventHooks.description: If any of the embed-source fields changed, dispatch RefreshEmbeddingJob.

owned.v1Status: SCHEMA-ONLY. Trait defined, macro reserves the column shape, but no active use in v1.

consumed.description: String primary keys backed by ULID.
Enables Laravel's default notification pipeline on the model.
The raw auditing trait wrapped by foundation's HasAuditable.
The raw versioning trait wrapped by foundation's HasVersions.

compositionExample.note: The canonical trait order every persistent model uses. Framework first, then workspaces boundary, then foundation, then domain-specific. Order matters — HasAuditable must land after HasUserstamps, and HasVersions must land after HasAuditable so the audit row references the userstamp and the version snapshot includes the audit.
compositionExample.code:
final class Branch extends Model implements BranchInterface, AuditableContract
{
    use HasFactory;             // framework
    use HasUlids;               // framework

    use BelongsToWorkspace;        // workspaces — adds workspace_id + global scope
    use BelongsToApplication;   // workspaces — adds application_id (when a boundary row)

    use HasSystemFlag;          // foundation
    use HasMetadata;            // foundation
    use HasUserstamps;          // foundation — MUST come before HasAuditable
    use HasAuditable;           // foundation — wraps owen-it/laravel-auditing
    use HasVersions;            // foundation — wraps mpociot/versionable; MUST come after HasAuditable
    use Sluggable;              // foundation — auto-fills slug on saving
    use Filterable;             // foundation — filterable() static contract
    use Sortable;               // foundation — sort_order + reorder helpers

    use HasImportable;          // transfer — opt into the import pipeline (optional)
    use HasExportable;          // transfer — opt into the export pipeline (optional)
    use HasSearchable;          // search   — opt into the search pipeline (optional)

    use SoftDeletes;            // framework — always last
}

blueprintExample.note: Migrations compose paired macros in the SAME order the model composes traits. Auditable, versionable, filterable are marker macros (no column changes) — they annotate intent for the CI cross-check + serve as anchoring documentation. Import / export / search markers moved out: transfer + search modules discover via PHP attributes, not migration macros.

traitOrderContract.rule: Framework first (HasFactory, HasUlids), then boundary traits (BelongsToApplication, BelongsToWorkspace), then foundation lifecycle traits in this exact order: HasSystemFlag, HasMetadata, HasUserstamps, HasAuditable, HasVersions, then indexer traits (Sluggable, Filterable, Sortable), then optional cross-module traits (transfer::HasImportable, transfer::HasExportable, search::HasSearchable, activity::HasActivityLog, audit::HasAudit), then SoftDeletes last.
why: HasUserstamps MUST come before HasAuditable so the userstamps are set before the audit row snapshots them.
HasAuditable MUST come before HasVersions so the audit row is written BEFORE the version snapshot captures the model state (otherwise the snapshot would miss the concurrent audit).
HasVersions MUST come after HasAuditable so a `revertToVersion` writes both a new audit row (the revert IS an audit-worthy mutation) and a new version snapshot (the new state).
Boundary traits come before lifecycle traits so BelongsToWorkspace's global scope is registered before HasAuditable tries to read the current workspace.
SoftDeletes goes last so its `deleting` hook doesn't shadow HasUserstamps' `deleting` hook.
traitOrderContract.enforcedBy: CI lint rule reads the composition order from the model AST and matches against this contract. Violations fail the build.
